10.argument: Bertrand gives q < 2p for every left boundary prime p, so any earlier composite k and winner w in the same gap satisfy w < q < 2p < 2k. Because d(w) < 10, the worst-case comparison is d(w) = 9. Thus L(w) > ((3-10)/2) ln(2k). Therefore L(k) < L(w) whenever k > 2^(10-3) = 128. The exact no-early-spoiler audit through p < 10^9 covers the remaining finite base k <= 128, so class 10 is closed.

notes: Residual frontier reconstructed from the 1e9 checkpoint directory by deduplicating 52,439 retained exact cases and recomputing local fields for the 12,099 rows with residual earlier divisor classes. Every reconstructed residual row has first_d4_offset = null. Observed residual gaps are all of size at most 22, and for D >= 36 the retained frontier uses only gap sizes 4 or 6. This run closes D in {10,14,18,20,22,26,27,28,30} by combining the universal Bertrand bound w < 2k with the exact p < 1e9 audit. Classes {36,40,42,44,50,52,54,56,60} remain finite-base obligations, but no counterexample is known: the exact audits through p < 1e9 and p < 5e9 both report zero earlier spoilers.
